zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor Beaumont於1100年出生在英國爾鄧翰 梅西時，她的父親Robert Beaumont54歲，母親Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois19歲。她於1124年與Hamon de Massey II在英國英格蘭爾鄧翰 梅西結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1157年在英國爾享年57歲，埋葬在英國爾。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor Beaumont was born in 1100, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, her father, Robert Beaumont, was 54 and her mother, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, was 19. She married Hamon de Massey II in 1124, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1157, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Eleanor Beaumont est née en 1100, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Beaumont, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Hamon de Massey II en 1124, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1157, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Cheshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor Beaumontが1100年にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで生まれた時，父Robert Beaumontは54歳，母Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandoisは19歳であった。1124年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイでHamon de Massey IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1157年にイギリスチェシュアイアで57歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor Beaumont(이)가 1100년 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Beaumont(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1124년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 던함 마시에서 Hamon de Massey II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1157년 영국 체실르에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor Beaumont родилась в 1100 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Beaumont было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Isabelle Capet Comtesse of Vermandois, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hamon de Massey II в 1124 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1157 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности чешир, Англия.
